name.en: Grafting tomato onto Lanka Sour rootstock

description.en: Graft the scion variety (e.g. HORDI Tomato hybrid 03) onto Lanka Sour rootstock to leverage its bacterial-wilt resistance. Research in Sri Lanka shows 86.67% healthy plant rate with this combination; the literature reports 85–95% survival for bacterial-wilt-susceptible tomato grafted on resistant rootstocks.

instructions.en: Raise rootstock (Lanka Sour) seedlings 2–3 days ahead of scion (HORDI Tomato hybrid 03 or other susceptible commercial variety). At 2–3 true-leaf stage, perform tube/cleft grafting. Heal grafts in a high-humidity chamber (~95% RH) at 25–28 °C for 5–7 days, then gradually harden off. Transplant only healthy, fully-knitted grafts.

description.quote: Seedlings grafted to resistant rootstocks such as Lanka Sour with HORDI Tomato hybrid 03 showed the highest healthy plant percentage (86.67%). Additionally, bacterial wilt susceptible tomato grafted on resistant rootstocks resulted in 85–95 per cent survival in Sri Lanka.

description.en: Biological control using *Trichoderma asperellum* — antagonistic soil fungus that suppresses *Ralstonia solanacearum* growth. In Sri Lankan trials, *T. asperellum* together with 1% Bordeaux mixture inhibited bacterial growth by 100% in vitro; treated plants showed significant reduction in bacterial density and no plant deaths versus untreated controls.

instructions.en: Apply *Trichoderma asperellum* formulation to nursery beds and again at transplanting as a soil drench, following the product manufacturer's dosage. Combine with field sanitation (clean tools, disease-free seed) and, where appropriate, a 1% Bordeaux mixture soil drench.

description.quote: Trichoderma asperellum and Bordeaux mixture (1%) inhibited the growth of Ralstonia solanacearum by 100%, with plants treated showing significant reduction in bacterial growth compared to control with no plant deaths.

name.en: Chlorothalonil 500 g/L SC multi-site contact fungicide

description.en: DOA-listed multi-site contact fungicide (chloronitrile group) for foliar fungal diseases of brinjal and other vegetables. Low resistance-development risk; suitable as a tank-mix partner or rotation partner with single-site systemic fungicides.

instructions.en: Foliar spray: 30 ml per 10 L water. Application rates: 950–1,200 ml/ha for low foliage, 1,500–1,800 ml/ha for high foliage. Repeat every 7–10 days.

safety_notes.en: PHI 14 days. Wear PPE. Toxic to aquatic organisms — do not apply near water bodies. Respiratory irritant.

instructions.quote: Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC is recommended at 30 ml per 10 liters of water, with application rates of 950-1200 ml per hectare for low foliage and 1500-1800 ml per hectare for high foliage, with 7-10 day application intervals and 14-day PHI. For specific vegetable crops, chlorothalonil 500g/l SC is recommended at 30 ml per 10 liters of water for brinjal disease management.

description.en: Para-pheromone male-lure trap. ME is a potent attractant that exploits male *Bactrocera dorsalis* pheromone chemistry — males ingest ME and convert it to sex-pheromone metabolites, so the lure doubles as both an attractant and a reproductive disruptor. Standard non-chemical tool for fruit-fly monitoring and mass trapping in Sri Lankan orchards.

instructions.en: Hang 4–8 ME traps per acre on the windward side of the orchard canopy at 1.5–2 m height. Recharge lure blocks every 6–8 weeks depending on product. Do NOT co-deploy with cue-lure in the same trap for *B. dorsalis* — ME + cue-lure mixtures reduce catch versus ME alone. Combine with sanitation: remove fallen and overripe fruit daily, bury 60 cm deep or bag and solarise.

description.quote: Traps baited with methyl eugenol and cue-lure (mixed or separate) captured significantly fewer B. dorsalis males than traps baited with methyl eugenol alone

instructions.quote: methyl-eugenol field traps have been used to collect adult fruit flies in Sri Lanka

description.en: DOA 2019-cited protectant dithiocarbamate fungicide for foliar fungal diseases (Cercospora, Mycosphaerella, Alternaria, Septoria leaf spots; anthracnose; early/late blight; downy mildew). Purely protective action — apply **before** disease establishes.

instructions.en: Foliar spray: dissolve 20 g per 10 L water. Application rates: 650–800 g/ha for low-foliage crops, 1,000–1,200 g/ha for high-foliage crops. Repeat at 7–10 day intervals. Tank-mix with a systemic fungicide for late-blight management.

safety_notes.en: PHI 14 days. Wear PPE — respirator, gloves, long sleeves. Avoid drift to water bodies. Moderate toxicity to fish.

instructions.quote: mancozeb 75% WG is recommended at 20 g per 10 liters of water, with application rates of 650-800 g per hectare for low foliage and 1000-1200 g per hectare for high foliage, with a 7-10 day application interval and 14-day PHI

chemical.quote: 20 g per 10 liters of water, with application rates of 650-800 g per hectare for low foliage and 1000-1200 g per hectare for high foliage, with a 7-10 day application interval and 14-day PHI
chemical.review_notes: PHI 14 days per DOA; agronomist must verify current ORP registration before publish (hard gate).

description.en: Broad-spectrum plant-derived biopesticide from *Azadirachta indica*. The active compound **azadirachtin** acts as an antifeedant, repellent, repugnant, and insect-growth-regulator — inhibits oviposition, disrupts moulting, and suppresses sperm production. Low residual, low toxicity to parasitoids/predators/honeybees, and biodegradable — making it compatible with IPM programmes that preserve natural enemies. Used across Sri Lankan vegetable, fruit, and field crops. Multiple DOA-referenced formulations on the market.

instructions.en: **Azadirachtin 1% EC**: 16 ml per 16 L tank (recommended DOA rate for leaf miner). **Neem seed kernel extract (NSKE) 5%**: crush 50 g neem seed kernel per 1 L water, soak overnight, strain, add a wetting agent, apply as foliar spray. Repeat every 7–14 days under active pest pressure. **Timing**: apply in the cool of early morning or late evening to minimise UV breakdown of azadirachtin. Best used **prophylactically at first sign of infestation** — not after populations peak. Rotate with other MoA groups.

description.quote: Neem (Azadirachta indica) belonging to the Meliaceae family has emerged as a highly potent bio-pesticide. The most prominent constituent of neem is azadirachtin, which has been established as a pivotal insecticidal ingredient. It acts as an antifeedant, repellent, and repugnant agent and induces sterility in insects by preventing oviposition and interrupting sperm production in males. Neem demonstrates strong antifeedant and insect metamorphosis disruptor properties besides lower residual limit and minimal toxicity to parasitoids, predators, and biocontrol agents.

instructions.quote: DOA recommends Azadiractin 1% EC at 16ml/16 liters of water

description.en: CRI-validated mass-trapping IPM protocol for *Rhynchophorus ferrugineus*. **4-methyl-5-nonanol (ferrugineol)** is the major aggregation pheromone. Ferrugineol + kairomone (1:3 ethyl acetate : ethanol) blend catches 1.4–2.2× more weevils than ferrugineol alone.

instructions.en: Use 5 L open-top plastic buckets. Bait with ferrugineol + pentanol lure and a fermenting food attractant (date palm pieces or sugarcane chunks in water). Hang buckets at **1.5 m height on coconut palm stems**. Deploy 1 trap per acre (more around recently damaged palms). Replace lure every 8–12 weeks (field effectiveness persists up to 12 weeks). Empty and clean traps weekly.

instructions.quote: 4-methyl-5-nonanol (ferrugineol), the major component of the aggregation pheromone of Rhynchophorus ferrugineus F., was evaluated as a lure for trapping of this pest in coconut plantations in Sri Lanka. 5L open plastic buckets baited with ferrugineol-pentanol and hung on coconut palm stems at a height of 1.5 meters catch significantly more adult weevils than other trap designs. the effectiveness of these lures can persist for up to 12 weeks under field conditions

description.quote: traps baited with the synthetic kairomone (1:3 ethyl acetate/ethanol) captured 1.4-2.2 times more total weevils than traps baited only with ferrugineol

name.en: Burnt paddy husk incorporation + certified seed

description.en: Foundational cultural practice DOA RRDI recommends for nearly every major rice disease: (1) use certified disease-free seed paddy; (2) apply burnt paddy husk at 250 kg per acre during land preparation; (3) avoid incorporating disease-infected straw.

instructions.en: During land preparation, broadcast 250 kg of burnt paddy husk per acre and incorporate into the soil. Source seed paddy from certified disease-free suppliers. Remove and compost infected crop residue away from the field rather than tilling it in.

instructions.quote: Use certified disease-free seed, add burnt paddy husk (250 kg/acre), avoid infected straw

name.en: Isoprothiolane 400 g/l EC for rice blast

instructions.en: Dissolve 20 ml in 16 l of water; apply 8–10 tanks per acre.

chemical.dosage: 20 ml per 16 l tank; 8–10 tanks per acre

safety_notes.en: Verify PHI against current ORP registration. Standard fungicide PPE.

instructions.quote: Isoprothiolane 400g/l EC: dissolve 20 ml in 16 l of water (8-10 tanks per acre)

chemical.quote: Isoprothiolane 400g/l EC: dissolve 20 ml in 16 l of water (8-10 tanks per acre)

name.en: Tebuconazole 250 g/l EC foliar spray for rice blast

description.en: Systemic triazole fungicide recommended by DOA RRDI for rice blast. Apply via knapsack sprayer dissolved in water.

instructions.en: Dissolve 10 ml in 16 l of water; apply 8–10 tanks per acre.

chemical.dosage: 10 ml per 16 l tank; 8–10 tanks per acre

safety_notes.en: Wear PPE; avoid drift to adjacent non-target crops. Honeybee protection: spray during non-flowering hours. Pre-harvest interval to be verified against current DOA registration before publication.

instructions.quote: Tebuconazole 250g/l EC: dissolve 10 ml in 16 l of water (8-10 tanks per acre)

chemical.quote: Tebuconazole 250g/l EC: dissolve 10 ml in 16 l of water (8-10 tanks per acre)
chemical.review_notes: PHI placeholder 30 days is conservative; agronomist must verify against current Office of the Registrar of Pesticides registration before publish — hard gate per CLAUDE.md.

description.en: Melanin-biosynthesis inhibitor specific for rice blast; penetrant with short systemic movement.

instructions.en: Dissolve 10 g in 16 l of water; apply 8–10 tanks per acre.

chemical.dosage: 10 g per 16 l tank; 8–10 tanks per acre

safety_notes.en: PHI placeholder — verify against current ORP registration. Wear PPE during mixing and application. Avoid drift.
